menu_navigation.description: The OmniPro 220 uses an LCD display with three knobs (Left, Main/Control, Right) and two buttons (Home, Back). Navigation is sequential — you move through screens by pressing the Main Control Knob.

controls.home_button: Returns to process selection screen from anywhere
controls.main_control_knob_turn: Scrolls through options on current screen
controls.main_control_knob_press: Selects current option / advances to next screen

MIG.name: Process Selection
MIG.display: MIG Stainless Steel (or MIG Steel, etc.)
MIG.action: Turn Main Knob to find MIG process, press to select

MIG.name: Polarity & Gas Confirmation
MIG.display: Shows cable diagram (- and +) with gas type (e.g. Stainless Tri-Mix, C25)
MIG.action: Plug cables per diagram. Connect gas. Set SCFH 20-30. Press Main Knob to confirm.

MIG.name: Wire Diameter & Material Thickness
MIG.action: Press Main Knob to confirm and go to weld screen

MIG.name: Auto Weld Settings (Main Weld Screen)
MIG.display: Top: Wire Feed Speed (in/min) and Voltage (V). Bottom: Process, Gas Type, wire diameter, material thickness. Run-In WFS, Inductance, Spot Timer shown.
MIG.action: This is the welding screen. Press Main Knob to enter Optional Settings.
MIG.note: White mark on setting line shows recommended value for your wire/thickness combo

MIG.name: Optional: Run-In WFS
MIG.action: Turn Main Knob to adjust. This sets wire speed before contact (% of preset WFS).

MIG.name: Optional: Inductance
MIG.action: Turn Main Knob to adjust. Increase = more fluid puddle, flatter bead. Decrease = colder puddle.

MIG.name: Optional: Spot Timer
MIG.action: Turn Main Knob to set timer for spot welding.

MIG.name: Optional: Recall Setting
MIG.action: Retrieve previously saved settings from 5 available slots.

MIG.name: Optional: Save Setting
MIG.action: Turn Main Knob to scroll 5 slots. Press to access OVERWRITE MEMORY SETTING. Turn to YES, press to save.

Flux-Cored.name: Process Selection
Flux-Cored.action: Turn Main Knob to find Flux-Cored process, press to select

Flux-Cored.name: Polarity & Gas Confirmation
Flux-Cored.display: Shows DCEN cable diagram — ground to +, wire feed to -. No gas required.
Flux-Cored.action: Plug cables per diagram. Press Main Knob to confirm.

Flux-Cored.name: Auto Weld Settings
Flux-Cored.display: Same layout as MIG but no gas type shown

TIG.name: Process Selection
TIG.action: Turn Main Knob to find TIG process, press to select

TIG.name: Polarity & Gas Confirmation
TIG.display: Shows DCEN cable diagram — ground to +, TIG torch to -. 100% Argon.
TIG.action: Plug cables per diagram. Connect 100% Argon. Set SCFH 10-25. Press to confirm.

TIG.name: Auto Weld Settings
TIG.display: Top-left: Amperage (e.g. 30 A). Top-right: ON/OFF toggle. Bottom: Process (TIG with Pedal), Gas (100% Ar), rod diameter, material thickness.
TIG.right_knob: Toggle ON/OFF — WARNING: turning ON energizes TIG Torch with Open Circuit Voltage
TIG.critical_warning: When Right Knob is set to ON, the welder is energized and OCV is present on the torch

Stick.name: Process Selection
Stick.action: Turn Main Knob to find Stick process, press to select

Stick.name: Electrode Type
Stick.action: Turn Main Knob to select electrode type (60xx or 70xx). Press to confirm.

Stick.name: Polarity Confirmation
Stick.display: Shows DCEP cable diagram — ground to -, electrode holder to +. No Gas Required.
Stick.action: Plug cables per diagram. Press to confirm.

Stick.name: Auto Weld Settings
Stick.display: Top-left: Amperage (e.g. 60 A). Top-right: ON/OFF toggle. Bottom: Process (Stick), Electrode Type (70XX), electrode diameter, material thickness, Hot Start, Arc Force.
Stick.right_knob: Toggle ON/OFF — WARNING: turning ON energizes Electrode Holder with OCV
Stick.critical_warning: When Right Knob is set to ON, the welder is energized

Stick.name: Optional: Hot Start
Stick.action: Adjust amperage boost at start of weld. Higher = easier arc ignition.

Stick.name: Optional: Arc Force
Stick.action: Adjust weld penetration and smoothness. Higher = more aggressive dig.

thermal_shutdown.display: Warning triangle icon with table: Problem | Remedy. Lists: Duty Cycle Exceeded, Low Voltage Input, High Voltage Input.
thermal_shutdown.behavior: Machine shuts down automatically. Returns to service after cooling. Keep Power Switch ON for fan cooling.
